title: Restore the Ledger safely
description: Validate recovery into a separate target before any explicitly authorized replacement of the authoritative Ledger. Implements G8 and UC-LEDGER-007.
rationale: A restore operation must not turn a recoverable problem into loss of the last authoritative copy.
story_want: restore a verified backup without risking the current Ledger
story_so_that: recovery is deliberate and provably complete
acceptance_criteria: Given a compatible verified backup and a separate empty target, when restore is requested, then the candidate is restored and validated without mutating the current authoritative Ledger.

Given a restored candidate, when validation completes, then every Durable Ledger State type, idempotency outcome, per-type count, relationship/cardinality rule, integrity result, and exact derived total matches the verified backup report.
Given any compatibility, integrity, permission, space, or validation failure, when restore ends, then the current Ledger remains authoritative and no partial replacement is activated.
Given a validated candidate and explicit replacement authorization, when activation succeeds, then the replacement is atomic and the prior Ledger remains available as the documented recovery point.
